perturbation.original_text: The term “ Representatives ” in the case of RHI shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, and with prior written consent of JDA (not to be unreasonably withheld), investment bankers, financial advisors, potential sources of capital or financing (debt or equity), agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of RHI. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage, Greenhill & Co, or Bain & Company to act as its consultant, investment banker or financial advisor in connection with a Possible Transaction or to discuss with, or engage, Deloitte as its advisor or accountant in connection with a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Representatives ” in the case of RHI shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, and with prior written consent of JDA (not to be unreasonably withheld), investment bankers, financial advisors, potential sources of capital or financing (debt or equity), agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of RHI. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any engagement of Greenhill & Co, Bain & Company, or Deloitte in connection with a Possible Transaction requires the explicit written consent of JDA, which may be withheld at JDA's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that engaging Greenhill & Co, Bain & Company, or Deloitte requires explicit written consent from JDA that can be withheld at JDA's sole discretion. This directly contradicts the original text which states that "nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the ability of RHI to discuss with, or engage" these entities for specific purposes, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions . (a) The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which is furnished on or after November 8, 2011 to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) on the Recipient's behalf in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives to the extent containing or which are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder, The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s or its Representatives possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that the source of such information was not known by the Recipient or its Representatives to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) was or is independently developed by the Recipient or the Recipient’s Representatives without the use of or reference to any Evaluation Material provided by or on behalf of Provider or its Representatives. (b) The term “ Representatives ” in the case of JDA shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, investment bankers, financial advisors, agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of JDA. The term “ Representatives ” in the case of RHI shall mean the directors, officers, employees, counsel, and with prior written consent of JDA (not to be unreasonably withheld), investment bankers, financial advisors, potential sources of capital or financing (debt or equity), agents, consultants, advisors, accountants or auditors of RHI. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any engagement of Greenhill & Co, Bain & Company, or Deloitte in connection with a Possible Transaction requires the explicit written consent of JDA, which may be withheld at JDA's sole discretion. (c) The term “ Person ” includes the media and any corporation, partnership, group, individual or other entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original allowance for RHI to engage Greenhill & Co, Bain & Company, or Deloitte without prior consent, now requiring explicit written consent from JDA. This creates a direct conflict within the 'Definitions' section itself (where both the original and changed text are located) as it first states no restriction and then states a restriction, thus meeting the definition of inconsistencies leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and each Party hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party understands and agrees that a preliminary agreement regarding a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties from the date of initial Evaluation Material exchange, and while not a definitive contract, creates a good-faith obligation to negotiate towards a final agreement. Each Party hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that no contract exists until a final definitive agreement is executed. The modified text introduces the concept of a preliminary agreement creating a good-faith obligation, which directly contradicts the initial statement that no contract exists before the final agreement, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Not a Transaction Agreement . Each Party understands and agrees that a preliminary agreement regarding a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties from the date of initial Evaluation Material exchange, and while not a definitive contract, creates a good-faith obligation to negotiate towards a final agreement. Each Party hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction. Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text states no contract exists until a final agreement. The changed text introduces a 'preliminary agreement' with 'good-faith obligation', contradicting the original stance and the contradicted text regarding legal obligations before a final agreement. The changed text creates an inconsistency by establishing some form of obligation which contradicts the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will no longer be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Recipient's obligations continue even after the Evaluation Material is returned or destroyed. The modified text completely reverses this, stating that the obligations cease upon return or destruction of the material. This creates an in-text contradiction about the duration of the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination of Discussions . If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the written request of the Provider for any reason, the Recipient will, and will direct its Representatives to, within five business days after receipt of such notice or request, destroy or return all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained (except that one copy may be maintained by outside legal counsel to the Recipient for archival purposes), and the term of this Agreement shall NOT be extended in case of non-compliance. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence. Upon the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will no longer be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that upon return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient is no longer bound by their obligations. However, the 'Contradicted Text' section explicitly states the Recipient will destroy or return all Evaluation Material but does not specify that obligations cease at that point. In fact, the 'Contradicted Text' is in the same location as the Changed Text. The Changed text directly contradicts the original intent that the obligations continue. The perturbation explanation clearly indicates that this change creates an in-text contradiction about the duration of the confidentiality obligations. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, arrangements and discussions between the Parties regarding such subject matter (including that certain Mutual Nondisclosure Agreement, dated November 8, 2011, between JDA Software Group, Inc. and RedPrairie Holding, Inc.).
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes a partial agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter hereof and does NOT supersede all prior agreements, understandings, arrangements and discussions between the Parties regarding such subject matter, and the prior Mutual Nondisclosure Agreement, dated November 8, 2011, between JDA Software Group, Inc. and RedPrairie Holding, Inc., remains in full effect.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause defines the current agreement as the *entire* agreement, superseding all previous ones. The modified version states that this agreement is only *partial* and the previous one still applies, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the scope of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes a partial agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter hereof and does NOT supersede all prior agreements, understandings, arrangements and discussions between the Parties regarding such subject matter, and the prior Mutual Nondisclosure Agreement, dated November 8, 2011, between JDA Software Group, Inc. and RedPrairie Holding, Inc., remains in full effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement is 'entire' and supersedes all prior agreements, while the changed text states it's 'partial' and does NOT supersede prior agreements, explicitly stating that a previous agreement remains in effect. The 'Contradicted Text' directly repeats the language of the 'Changed Text'. The explanation given and the definitions for inconsistencies given state that the original version and modified version contradict the agreement's scope. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
